title: Robin Hood

evidence.note: Published 1922; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

trademark.note: Robin Hood is a public-domain folk legend, but 'Robin Hood'-branded franchises and logos may carry independent trademark rights. Undetermined here; check marks before branded use.

watch.label: Internet Archive — Robin Hood (1922), full film

editorial: Robin Hood is a 1922 American silent adventure directed by Allan Dwan and produced by and starring Douglas Fairbanks, released through United Artists on a then-record budget with monumental castle sets. Copyrighted as 'Douglas Fairbanks in Robin Hood,' it was the first motion picture to have a Hollywood premiere and cemented Fairbanks's reputation for large-scale costume spectacle. Published in 1922, the film print's 95-year US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry.
